action.reasoning: Observation: Baseline 0.2436 with severe forgetting (task 9 at 0.812, earlier tasks <0.20). Analysis: The classifier is heavily biased toward most recent classes — classic CIL bias. Need: (1) larger memory buffer, (2) bias correction (e.g., normalize classifier weights / use cosine), (3) more epochs. The DER++/iCaRL approach with NCM (nearest class mean) classification often works much better. Let me move to a stronger approach: increase replay memory to 2000 (20/class), use cosine classifier, longer training, and importantly use NCM classification at test time to avoid bias. Decision: Rewrite training to use stronger replay + KD, store class means, and predict via NCM.
